name: Update BankAccReconTest.Codeunit.al
description: Changes in iteration 2 that received 2 comment(s)
pr_title: Performance improvement for total outstanding amounts in bank rec posting

target_comments.comment:
It looks like we need this kind of SIFT index on Bank Account Ledger Entry (we don't have it)

"Bank Account No.", "Posting Date", "Statement No", "Closed at Date", "Statement Status", Reversed, Open 
SumIndexFields =  Amount
target_comments.author: Djordje Cenic

target_comments.comment: It is more efficient to line up the valid values with OR (|) than say <> InvalidOne. It may be counterintuitive, but according to Torben Wind, it is like that. In the <> case on an enum, even if we have a SIFT index for Amount (which we need to add), having a <> InvalidOne will make it do a full index scan (performs like a table scan with a sum - in other words, no help), whereas a sequence of ORs makes the server fetch the precalculated sums for the individual enum members, much more efficient.
target_comments.author: Djordje Cenic

name: Update PaymentToleranceManagement.Codeunit.al
description: Changes in iteration 1 that received 8 comment(s)
pr_title: Bug 613035: Performance regression in Payment Tolerance management codeunit

target_comments.comment:
We are setting a filter on ("Document No.", "Vendor No.", Open). VendorLedgerEntry table doesn't have an index that has both "Document No." and "Vendor No.". This will potentially be a long running query.
- We have called AppliedVendLedgEntry.SetCurrentKey("Document No."); - this means ORDER BY Document No
- then we add SetRange on "Vendor No", Open and "Document No." (this means WHERE clause on these three fields)
We have
key(Key4; "Document No.")
key(Key6; "Vendor No.", Open, Positive, "Due Date", "Currency Code")
and it is unknown which one will the SQL Server pick. If it picks key4, and if Document No. are unique across vendors, then we are fine.
But if it picks key6, and if this Vendor has a lot of open entries, then it's like a table scan. Remember, we have some LiveSite customers that have only one vendor and a lot of open vendor ledger entries for that one vendor.

Maybe it would be a good idea to add a key that has both "Document No.", Open and "Vendor No.". Then there is a high chance that SQL Server will pick that one.
target_comments.author: Djordje Cenic

target_comments.comment:
Based on the SetCurrentKey (meaning ORDER BY) and the fields that we have set the range, SQL Server will most likely pick one of these two keys
        key(Key6; "Customer No.", Open, Positive, "Due Date", "Currency Code")
        key(Key17; "Customer No.", "Applies-to ID", Open, Positive, "Due Date")
But none of them contain Document No.
if there is a situation where this is the one and only customer (and there are cases like that in Live Site, businesses have only one customer) with a lot of open ledger entries, then searching for Document No. is like a table scan - it will be a long running query.

Maybe we should have a key on Cust Ledger Entry that includes "Customer No.", "Document No", Open (no need for column Positive in this scenario at all).
target_comments.author: Djordje Cenic

target_comments.comment: ReadIsolation UpdLock to use instance-level isolation.
target_comments.author: Djordje Cenic

target_comments.comment: This will be Select Top 50. It might as well be FindFirst, because we are updating just one.
target_comments.author: Djordje Cenic

target_comments.comment: we don't need this call that will lock all instances of this record - ModifyAll will lock what it needs to lock. We could say ReadIsolation UpdLock to make sure we are using instance-level locking.
target_comments.author: Djordje Cenic

target_comments.comment:
"Applies-to ID" instead of Positive here.
Let's not bring Positive into the game for no reason. We want SQL Server to pick
        key(Key17; "Customer No.", "Applies-to ID", Open, Positive, "Due Date")
If we throw in Positive into the game for no reason, it might get confused and pick a key that has no Applies-to ID, e.g. this one
        key(Key6; "Customer No.", Open, Positive, "Due Date", "Currency Code")

If that happens, and if there is only one customer with a lot of open ledger entries, searching for Applies to ID will be like a table scan.
target_comments.author: Djordje Cenic

target_comments.comment: ReadIsolation UpdLock to make sure only this instance is locked.
target_comments.author: Djordje Cenic

target_comments.comment: Let's not lock all instances of this record (just ReadIsolation UpdLock). ModifyAll uses UpdLock anyway.
target_comments.author: Djordje Cenic

MaxLength = 255, because
Outlook Subject Line Limits
- Maximum allowed: 255 characters for the subject line in Outlook and Exchange environments.
- Exceeding the limit:
- If you try to send an email with a subject longer than 255 characters, Outlook may truncate it or fail to send depending on how the email is being composed (e.g., via Outlook client, webmail, or programmatically via APIs).

target_comments.comment:
Vauw. We are basically iterating over all customers and their open entries.... This must take forever if you have 1mill customers.....
How about directly looping through open CustomerLedgerEntries, and then in the loop, whenever the customer changes, we look up the customer and check if they are blocked and if so, skip(continue. That must be a lot faster. Think of 1 mill. customers, 10 mill. customer ledger entries of which perhaps only 1000 are open.....

+            "Rolled-up Mfg. Ovhd Cost" +=

+            Write-Log "Breaking Change Validation is disabled."
+            return ("AppSourceCop", "CodeCop", "UICop", "PTECop")
+        }
+
         # Should we restore this package outside this?
         Import-Module "$($ENV:INETROOT)\Eng\Core\Helpers\ExtensionsV2\GuardingV2ExtensionsHelper.psm1"
pr_title: [Agent Builder] Rename AL objects from Playground Agent to Custom Agent + introduce IsPrivatePreview concept

target_comments.comment:
I introduced a new concept here to control when to disable the breaking change validation. However other approaches could be considered:
- Being explicit and calling it disable validation - although not sure we want to expose it this way.
- Re-using IsInternal which is used by apps going through the same private preview motion or are planning to say internal - and would benefit from more flexibility, but I also see some system app modules and I fear relaxing validation for them would be bad.
target_comments.author: Quentin Tresontani

name: Update MTDTestReturnsWebService.Codeunit.al
description: Changes in iteration 26 that received 1 comment(s)
pr_title: [Security] OAuth 2.0 Setup - Table is public with public procedure to get token

target_comments.comment:
This was a test instability unrelated to the change, but I have to fix it or else it fails in buddybuild. The problem was the the test SubmitVATReturns_ExpiredToken which runs just before, leaves the magic number 333333344 in CompanyInformation."VAT Registration No.". This magic number tells the mock service: return 401 unauthorized.
Then when the test SubmitVATReturns_Timeout runs after, it sets '' to this magic field, meaning 'no change', which means mock service will again return 401 Unauthorized, making the test fail.
The fix was to set magic number 333333339, which means response 201 Created (I created the VAT Return after retrying with a fresh token).
target_comments.author: Djordje Cenic

target_comments.comment: With this, we can change the prompt to postpone the 'account missing' intervention to the final step, and keep the line correction accuracy tests.
target_comments.author: Djordje Cenic

name: Update LibraryPayablesAgent.Codeunit.al
description: Changes in iteration 3 that received 1 comment(s)
pr_title: [Payables Agent] Don't request intervention when a line is missing an account number

target_comments.comment:
Even though the prompt tells the agent to raise an assistance request that starts with 'The draft', sometimes it judges that this is grammatically incorrect and starts the request for assistance with 'Draft'. Therefore, we use LowerCase here, or the test just skips this (doesn't recognize it) and fails.
I understand from Jose that moving away from these hardcoded snippets in tests is on a to do list.
target_comments.author: Djordje Cenic

name: Update LibraryPayablesAgent.Codeunit.al
description: Changes in iteration 4 that received 1 comment(s)
pr_title: [Payables Agent] Don't request intervention when a line is missing an account number

target_comments.comment: This is needed because sometimes the agent enriches the message 'Please review the draft before purchase document is created.' and puts in extra details like 'Please review the purchase document draft for **vendor** such-and-such before purchase document is created.', so the test thinks that this is a request for attention that requires updating the vendor, but fails because the test data set has nothing about the vendor.
target_comments.author: Djordje Cenic

name: Update bcartifacts-partnerartifacts.yml
description: Changes in iteration 3 that received 1 comment(s)
diff:
diff --git a/.pipelines/bcartifacts-partnerartifacts.yml b/.pipelines/bcartifacts-partnerartifacts.yml
index 109d579..30a773c 100644
--- a/.pipelines/bcartifacts-partnerartifacts.yml
+++ b/.pipelines/bcartifacts-partnerartifacts.yml
@@ -107,7 +107,7 @@ extends:
             ArtifactsVersionOverride: '$(Build.BuildNumber)'
 
     - stage: NuGetGeneration
-      displayName: NuGet Package Generation
+      displayName: NuGet Package Generation (MSApps)
       jobs:
       - job: NuGetGenerationJob
         displayName: NuGet Package Generation
@@ -169,9 +169,72 @@ extends:
             targetPath: '$(ob_outputDirectory)'
             artifact: drop_NuGetGeneration_NuGetGenerationJob
 
+    - stage: NuGetGenerationSymbols
+      displayName: NuGet Package Generation (MSSymbols)
+      jobs:
+      - job: NuGetGenerationSymbolsJob
+        displayName: NuGet Package Generation (MSSymbols)
+        pool:
+          type: windows
+          isCustom: true
+          name: 'dynamics-bc-managed-xl'
+        variables:
+          - name: ob_outputDirectory
+            value: '$(Build.ArtifactStagingDirectory)/ONEBRANCH_ARTIFACT'
+          - name: ob_sdl_codeSignValidation_excludes
+            value: -|**\*ALLanguage.vsix; # Excluding ALLanguage.vsix extension which is scanned in Platform-ModernDev and Platform-Core
+          - name: ob_git_fetchTags
+            value: false
+        steps:
+        # Download BuildMetadata artifact
+        - download: _NAV-OfficialBuild
+          artifact: BuildMetadata
+          displayName: "Download BuildMetadata artifact"
+        # Download the ReleaseTools package needed for preparing the DVDs for release
+        - task: DownloadPackage@1
+          displayName: 'Download ReleaseTools package'
+          inputs:
+            packageType: 'nuget'
+            feed: '/DynamicsSMBPackages'
+            definition: 'Microsoft.BusinessCentral.SharedInfrastructure.ReleaseTools'
+            version: 'latest'
+            downloadPath: '$(System.ArtifactsDirectory)'
+        # Download the Development Tools package needed for generating NuGet packages
+        - task: DownloadPackage@1
+          displayName: 'Download Development Tools package'
+          inputs:
+            packageType: 'nuget'
+            feed: '/DynamicsSMBPackages'
+            definition: 'Microsoft.Dynamics.BusinessCentral.Development.Tools'
+            version: 'latest'
+            downloadPath: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)'
+        # Generate NuGet packages
+        - task: PowerShell@2
+          displayName: 'Generate NuGet packages'
+          env:
+            AZURE_DEVOPS_EXT_PAT: $(System.AccessToken) # Used for downloading universal packages within the script
+          inputs:
+            pwsh: true
+            targetType: 'filePath' 
+            filePath: '$(System.ArtifactsDirectory)/tools/MSFeedPublishing/GenerateNugetPackages.ps1'
+            arguments: "-ArtifactsManifest '$(Pipeline.Workspace)/_NAV-OfficialBuild/BuildMetadata/BuildArtifactsManifest.json'  `
+                        -LastModifiedManifest '$(Pipeline.Workspace)/_NAV-OfficialBuild/BuildMetadata/AppsLastModifiedVersions.json'  ` 
+                        -ArtifactsDirectory '$(ob_outputDirectory)'  `
+                        -TempDirectory '$(Agent.TempDirectory)'  `
+                        -SymbolsOnly `
+                        -Incremental:([bool]::Parse('${{ parameters.PublishOnlyChangedApps }}'))"
+
+        # Publish the generated NuGet packages as pipeline artifacts
+        - task: PublishPipelineArtifact@1
+          displayName: 'Publish Artifact'
+          condition: succeeded()
+          inputs:
+            targetPath: '$(ob_outputDirectory)'
+            artifact: drop_NuGetGenerationSymbols_NuGetGenerationSymbolsJob
+
     - stage: NuGetPublishing
       displayName: NuGet Package Publish
-      dependsOn: NuGetGeneration
+      dependsOn: [NuGetGeneration, NuGetGenerationSymbols]
       condition: succeeded()
       jobs:
       - job: NuGetPublishJob
@@ -189,11 +252,22 @@ extends:
         # Download the NuGet packages generated in the previous stage
         - download: current
           artifact: drop_NuGetGeneration_NuGetGenerationJob
-          displayName: 'Download NuGet packages'
+          displayName: 'Download NuGet packages (MSApps)'
+        # Download the NuGet packages generated in the previous stage
+        - download: current
+          artifact: drop_NuGetGenerationSymbols_NuGetGenerationSymbolsJob
+          displayName: 'Download NuGet packages (MSSymbols)'
         # Publish the NuGet packages to the specified feed
         - template: nuget-push.yml@PipelineTemplates
           parameters:
             PackagesPath: '$(Pipeline.Workspace)/drop_NuGetGeneration_NuGetGenerationJob'
             ServiceConnectionId: c281a868-b013-4ab7-88d9-33f56b9597c8 # NAVDocker-PartnerArtifacts-NugetPublisher
             FeedUri: 'https://dynamicssmb2.pkgs.visualstudio.com/DynamicsBCPublicFeeds/_packaging/TestFeedMSApps/nuget/v3/index.json' # Keeping the TestFeedMSApps feed for now. Will be changed later.
+            allowPackageConflicts: true
+        # Publish the NuGet packages to the specified feed
+        - template: nuget-push.yml@PipelineTemplates
+          parameters:
+            PackagesPath: '$(Pipeline.Workspace)/drop_NuGetGenerationSymbols_NuGetGenerationSymbolsJob'
+            ServiceConnectionId: c281a868-b013-4ab7-88d9-33f56b9597c8 # NAVDocker-PartnerArtifacts-NugetPublisher
+            FeedUri: 'https://dynamicssmb2.pkgs.visualstudio.com/DynamicsBCPublicFeeds/_packaging/TestFeedMSSymbols/nuget/v3/index.json' # Keeping the TestFeedMSApps feed for now. Will be changed later.
             allowPackageConflicts: true

     .EXAMPLE

+
+function Add-CopilotCapability
+(
+    [string] $ServerInstance = (Get-NavServerDefaultInstanceName),
+    [int] $Capability
+)
+{
+    Get-NAVTenant -ServerInstance $ServerInstance | ForEach-Object {
+        $copilotSettingsTable = "[$($_.DatabaseName)].[dbo].[Copilot Settings`$$SystemAppId]"
+        $capabilityConfigured = ($(Invoke-Sqlcmd -ServerInstance localhost -Query ("SELECT COUNT(Capability) AS Count FROM $copilotSettingsTable WHERE Capability = '$Capability'")).Count) -eq 1
+        if (!$capabilityConfigured)
+        {
+            Write-Log ("Inserting capability $Capability in $($_.DatabaseName).") -ForegroundColor yellow
+            Invoke-Sqlcmd -ServerInstance localhost -Query ("INSERT INTO $copilotSettingsTable ([Capability], [App Id], [Availability], [Publisher], [Status], [Learn More Url], [Service Type], [Billing Type]) VALUES ('$Capability', '$SystemAppId', 0, 'Microsoft', 0, 'https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2255821', '0', '0')")
+        }
+    }
+}

+            definition: 'Microsoft.Dynamics.BusinessCentral.Development.Tools'
+            version: 'latest'
+            downloadPath: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)'
+        # Generate NuGet packages
+        - task: PowerShell@2
+          displayName: 'Generate NuGet packages'
+          env:
+            AZURE_DEVOPS_EXT_PAT: $(System.AccessToken) # Used for downloading universal packages within the script
+          inputs:
+            pwsh: true
+            targetType: 'filePath' 
+            filePath: '$(System.ArtifactsDirectory)/tools/MSFeedPublishing/GenerateNugetPackages.ps1'
+            arguments: "-ArtifactsManifest '$(Pipeline.Workspace)/_NAV-OfficialBuild/BuildMetadata/BuildArtifactsManifest.json'  `
+                        -LastModifiedManifest '$(Pipeline.Workspace)/_NAV-OfficialBuild/BuildMetadata/AppsLastModifiedVersions.json'  ` 
+                        -ArtifactsDirectory '$(ob_outputDirectory)'  `
+                        -TempDirectory '$(Agent.TempDirectory)'  `
+                        -Incremental:([bool]::Parse('${{ parameters.PublishOnlyChangedApps }}'))"
+        # Publish the generated NuGet packages as pipeline artifacts
+        - task: PublishPipelineArtifact@1
+          displayName: 'Publish Artifact'
+          condition: succeeded()
+          inputs:
+            targetPath: '$(ob_outputDirectory)'
+            artifact: drop_NuGetGeneration_NuGetGenerationJob
+
+    - stage: NuGetPublishing
+      displayName: NuGet Package Publish
+      dependsOn: NuGetGeneration
+      condition: succeeded()
+      jobs:
+      - job: NuGetPublishJob
+        displayName: NuGet Package Publishing
+        pool:
+          type: windows
+        variables:
+          - name: ob_git_fetchTags
+            value: false
+          - name: ob_outputDirectory
+            value: '$(Build.ArtifactStagingDirectory)/ONEBRANCH_ARTIFACT'
+          - name: ob_sdl_codeSignValidation_excludes
+            value: -|**\*ALLanguage.vsix; # Excluding ALLanguage.vsix extension which is scanned in Platform-ModernDev and Platform-Core
+        steps:
+        # Download the NuGet packages generated in the previous stage
+        - download: current
+          artifact: drop_NuGetGeneration_NuGetGenerationJob
+          displayName: 'Download NuGet packages'
+        # Publish the NuGet packages to the specified feed
+        - template: nuget-push.yml@PipelineTemplates
+          parameters:
+            PackagesPath: '$(Pipeline.Workspace)/drop_NuGetGeneration_NuGetGenerationJob'
+            ServiceConnectionId: c281a868-b013-4ab7-88d9-33f56b9597c8 # NAVDocker-PartnerArtifacts-NugetPublisher
+            FeedUri: 'https://dynamicssmb2.pkgs.visualstudio.com/DynamicsBCPublicFeeds/_packaging/TestFeedMSApps/nuget/v3/index.json' # Keeping the TestFeedMSApps feed for now. Will be changed later.
+            allowPackageConflicts: true
\ No newline at end of file
pr_title: [Nugetizing BC] Adapt BCInsider pipeline to publish NuGet packages
